    ╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
    │  Tier 5 — Cannot generate reliably                                   │
    │  Tool: sort  Confidence: 31%                                         │
    │  Reason: no Bioconda entry, no Dockerfile, ambiguous I/O structure   │
    ╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

    Summary: 2 functionalities found — 1 can be generated (Tier 1–4), 1 needs manual work (Tier 5)

  Notes:
  - No container resolution, no inference, no file generation.
  - Summary line always printed last: "{total} found — {generatable} can be generated, {skipped} need manual work"
  - --json flag suppresses all Rich output and prints only the JSON array to stdout.
    This makes assess-only composable in shell scripts and CI pipelines.

  containers SOURCE [--functionality NAME] — ingest + discover +
  container discovery for one or all functionalities. Print all available options.
  Accepts --json flag to print structured JSON.
  Accepts --functionality NAME to inspect options for a specific functionality only.
  Never writes files. Exit code 0.

  Annotated transcript for containers (single functionality):

    $ code-to-module containers https://github.com/lab/mytool --functionality align

    📂 Repo scan: 4 files | Dockerfile ✓

    ⠋ Scanning for functionalities...
    ⠋ Discovering containers for: align...

    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │  📦 Container options for align                                      │
    ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    │  [1] ★ BioContainers          (default for Tier 1)                  │
    │        quay.io/biocontainers/bwa:0.7.17--h5bf99c6_8                 │
    │                                                                      │
    │  [2]   Dockerfile in repo                                            │
    │        ghcr.io/lab/mytool:latest                                     │
    │        ⚠ Build and push before nf-core submission                   │
    │                                                                      │
    │  [3]   Stub (fill in manually)                                       │
    │        quay.io/biocontainers/align:TODO--TODO                        │
    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    To use a specific option:
      code-to-module convert SOURCE --container biocontainers
      code-to-module convert SOURCE --container dockerfile
      code-to-module convert SOURCE --container stub

  Annotated transcript for containers (all functionalities, --json):

    $ code-to-module containers https://github.com/lab/mytool --json

    [
      {
        "functionality": "align",
        "tier": 1,
        "default": "biocontainers",
        "options": [
          {"source": "biocontainers", "docker_url": "quay.io/biocontainers/bwa:0.7.17--h5bf99c6_8",
           "singularity_url": "https://depot.galaxyproject.org/singularity/bwa:0.7.17--h5bf99c6_8",
           "is_default": true, "warnings": []},
          {"source": "dockerfile", "docker_url": "ghcr.io/lab/mytool:latest",
           "singularity_url": "docker://ghcr.io/lab/mytool:latest",
           "is_default": false,
           "warnings": ["Build and push before nf-core submission"]},
          {"source": "stub", "docker_url": "quay.io/biocontainers/align:TODO--TODO",
           "singularity_url": "https://depot.galaxyproject.org/singularity/align:TODO--TODO",
           "is_default": false, "warnings": []}
        ]
      }
    ]

  Notes:
  - --functionality filters to a single FunctionalitySpec before container discovery.
    Without it, discovery runs for all detected functionalities and output is grouped.
  - --json output is one JSON array, one object per functionality.
  - The Rich table and the --json output contain the same information.
  - ★ marks the default (is_default=True) in Rich output; "is_default": true in JSON.

  update-standards   — check/apply standards schema update
  test MODULE_PATH   — stub (implemented in Prompt 20)
  fix MODULE_PATH    — stub (implemented in Prompt 21)
  review MODULE_PATH — stub (implemented in Prompt 22)

Tests using Click's CliRunner:
  - test_single_functionality: simple_script.py → no menu shown, one module generated
  - test_multi_functionality_interactive: repo_click_multi/ → mock input "1,2" →
    two modules generated in separate subdirectories
  - test_all_functionalities_flag: --all-functionalities → all selected without menu
  - test_functionalities_flag: --functionalities "align" → only align generated
  - test_dry_run_multi: repo with 3 functionalities, --dry-run → prints "Would generate 3"
  - test_tier5_skipped: one functionality is Tier 5 → skipped, others generated,
    exit code 0 (partial success) with warning message
  - test_container_per_functionality: multi-script repo → container.resolve called
    once per selected functionality
  - test_no_functionalities_selected_exits: --functionalities "nonexistent" → exit code 2
  Mock all external calls (Anthropic, httpx, gitpython, nf-core, nf-test).

docs.title: Prompt 16 — End-to-End Tests with Real Fixtures
docs.text:
Goal: Create all test fixtures referenced throughout the tutorial and write end-to-end tests covering single and multi-functionality paths, all container strategies, and the full generate cycle.

--- FIXTURE SETUP ---

1. Single-functionality fixtures (container path testing):

   tests/fixtures/repo_with_dockerfile/aligner.py
     A simple BWA wrapper with click CLI: one command "align" that takes reads + reference,
     runs bwa mem, outputs a BAM file.

   tests/fixtures/repo_with_dockerfile/Dockerfile:
FROM quay.io/biocontainers/bwa:0.7.17--h5bf99c6_8
LABEL maintainer="test"
COPY aligner.py /usr/local/bin/aligner.py
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/aligner.py

   tests/fixtures/repo_with_envyml/filter.py
     A VCF filter script using argparse (single command, no subcommands).

   tests/fixtures/repo_with_envyml/environment.yml:
name: vcf-filter
channels:
  - conda-forge
  - bioconda
dependencies:
  - python=3.10
  - bcftools=1.17

   tests/fixtures/repo_with_both/samtools_wrapper.sh
     A bash script wrapping samtools — single command, no case statement.

   tests/fixtures/repo_with_both/Dockerfile:
FROM ubuntu:22.04
RUN apt-get install -y samtools

   tests/fixtures/repo_no_container/trimmer.sh:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
INPUT=$1; OUTPUT=$2
awk '{ print }' "$INPUT" > "$OUTPUT"

2. Multi-functionality fixtures (discovery path testing):

   tests/fixtures/repo_click_multi/mytool.py
     A click app with three commands: align, sort, index.
     Each command has typed arguments (reads: Path, reference: Path, output: Path).
     Each has a one-line docstring describing its purpose.

   tests/fixtures/repo_argparse_subcommands/tool.py
     An argparse script with add_subparsers() defining "filter" and "merge" subcommands.
     Each subparser has add_argument() calls for inputs and outputs.

   tests/fixtures/repo_shell_case/pipeline.sh:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
CMD=$1; shift
case "$CMD" in
  align)  bwa mem "$@" ;;
  sort)   samtools sort "$@" ;;
  help)   echo "Usage: pipeline.sh {align|sort}" ;;
  *)      echo "Unknown command: $CMD"; exit 1 ;;
esac

   tests/fixtures/repo_multi_scripts/:
     align.py  — standalone script, argparse, takes reads + ref, outputs BAM
     sort.py   — standalone script, argparse, takes BAM, outputs sorted BAM
     filter.py — standalone script, argparse, takes VCF, outputs filtered VCF
     (no __init__.py, no utils.py — all are top-level independent scripts)

--- END-TO-END TESTS in tests/test_end_to_end.py ---

Scenario A — Single file, no subcommands → SINGLE_SCRIPT discovery, DOCKERFILE container:
  - ingest(repo_with_dockerfile/) → CodeSource
  - discover() → DiscoveryResult, is_single_functionality=True
  - No selection menu shown
  - assess() → Tier 3
  - container discovery returns DOCKERFILE as default
  - generate() creates module in tmp_path/aligner/
  - Assert: main.nf exists, container block uses "docker://" URL
  - Assert: Dockerfile in output directory
  - Assert: meta.yml is valid YAML with required fields

Scenario B — environment.yml, Tier 3 → GENERATED_FROM_ENVYML:
  - Single file, no subcommands, environment.yml present
  - container discovery returns GENERATED_FROM_ENVYML and STUB
  - generate() creates Dockerfile with "mambaforge" content
  - Assert: generated Dockerfile in output dir

Scenario C — repo_with_both + Tier 1 → BIOCONTAINERS default:
  - Mock Bioconda API returning samtools version info
  - Tier 1 assessment
  - container.select() with no_interaction=True → BIOCONTAINERS
  - Assert: main.nf uses quay.io/biocontainers URL

Scenario D — --container flag overrides default:
  - repo_with_both, Tier 1 (would default to BioContainers)
  - container_flag="dockerfile" → DOCKERFILE selected
  - Assert: Dockerfile URL in main.nf

Scenario E — No container, Bioconda 404, Tier 3 → STUB:
  - Mock Bioconda 404
  - Assert: main.nf contains TODO container lines
  - Assert: warning printed

Scenario F — Multi-functionality click repo → 3 modules generated:
  - ingest(repo_click_multi/) → CodeSource
  - discover() → 3 FunctionalitySpecs (CLICK_DECORATOR), is_single_functionality=False
  - select_functionalities() with no_interaction=True → all 3 pre-selected
  - For each: assess(), infer_module_spec_sync(), generate()
  - Assert: 3 separate output directories created (align/, sort/, index/)
  - Assert: each main.nf has correct process_name (MYTOOL_ALIGN, MYTOOL_SORT, MYTOOL_INDEX)
  - Assert: process names do NOT conflict with each other

Scenario G — Multi-functionality, user selects subset:
  - repo_click_multi/, --functionalities "align,sort"
  - Only 2 modules generated
  - Assert: index/ directory NOT created
  - Assert: align/ and sort/ directories created

Scenario H — Mixed tiers in multi-functionality repo:
  - Mock assess() to return Tier 1 for "align", Tier 5 for "sort"
  - Assert: align/ generated, sort/ skipped with warning
  - Assert: exit code 0 (partial success, not failure)
  - Assert: final summary shows "1 generated, 1 skipped (Tier 5)"

For all scenarios: mock infer_module_spec_sync and all httpx calls.
Assert: generated main.nf parses as text, meta.yml loads as valid YAML,
        container URLs in main.nf match selected ContainerOption.docker_url.

Run: pytest tests/test_end_to_end.py -v
Fix any failures before proceeding.

docs.title: Prompt 17 — Post-Generate Quick Lint
docs.text:
Goal: Run a lightweight structural check immediately after convert finishes, before the user runs nf-test. This is intentionally narrow — it catches obvious generation errors (empty container, missing ext.args) that can be fixed without running nf-test. It does NOT duplicate the full lint pass in the test command (Prompt 20), which runs nf-core modules lint --json and classifies failures into Classes A/B/C.

The relationship between the two lint passes: - Prompt 17 (post-generate) — fires immediately after file creation; fast; catches structural problems that mean the module will definitely fail lint. Blocks exit with a warning but does not attempt fixes. - Prompt 20 (test command) — full lint + nf-test run invoked explicitly by the user; produces a TestReport consumed by fix. This is the authoritative pre-submission check.

Add src/code_to_module/quick_lint.py.

Export:
  def quick_lint(module_dir: Path, standards: Standards) -> list[QuickLintWarning]

  QuickLintWarning(
      severity: Literal["error", "warning"],
      check: str,       ← short identifier e.g. "missing_container"
      message: str,     ← human-readable description
      suggestion: str,  ← one-line fix hint
  )

Checks (all rule-based, no subprocess, no LLM):

  "missing_container":
    Read main.nf. If container line contains "TODO" or is empty → error.
    Suggestion: "Run with --container biocontainers or check Dockerfile is accessible."

  "missing_ext_args":
    Read main.nf. If "task.ext.args" not present in script: block → error.
    Use standards.ext_args_pattern for the expected string.
    Suggestion: "Add: def args = task.ext.args ?: '' to the script: block."

  "missing_ext_prefix":
    Read main.nf. If module has file outputs AND "task.ext.prefix" not present → warning.
    Use standards.ext_prefix_pattern.
    Suggestion: "Add: def prefix = task.ext.prefix ?: '${meta.id}' for named outputs."

  "wrong_label":
    Read main.nf. If label value is not in standards.valid_labels → error.
    Suggestion: f"Valid labels: {', '.join(standards.valid_labels)}"

  "missing_versions_topic":
    Read main.nf. If "topic: 'versions'" not present AND standards.versions_use_topic_channels → error.
    Suggestion: "Add topic: 'versions' to the versions emit channel."

  "meta_yml_missing_field":
    Read meta.yml. Check all standards.meta_yml_required_fields are present as top-level keys.
    For each missing field → warning.
    Suggestion: f"Add '{field}:' section to meta.yml."

In cli.py, after generate() and BEFORE printing next steps (unless --no-lint flag):
  warnings = quick_lint(module_dir, standards)
  if not warnings:
      console.print("[green]✓ Quick lint passed[/green]")
  else:
      errors = [w for w in warnings if w.severity == "error"]
      warnings_only = [w for w in warnings if w.severity == "warning"]
      for w in errors:
          console.print(f"[red]✗ {w.check}:[/red] {w.message}")
          console.print(f"  [dim]→ {w.suggestion}[/dim]")
      for w in warnings_only:
          console.print(f"[yellow]⚠ {w.check}:[/yellow] {w.message}")
          console.print(f"  [dim]→ {w.suggestion}[/dim]")
      if errors:
          console.print("[red]Quick lint found errors — run 'code-to-module fix' after reviewing.[/red]")

Tests in tests/test_quick_lint.py:
  - test_passes_clean_module: valid main.nf with all required elements → empty list
  - test_missing_container: main.nf with "container TODO" → QuickLintWarning severity=error
  - test_missing_ext_args: no task.ext.args → error
  - test_missing_ext_prefix_with_outputs: file outputs but no task.ext.prefix → warning
  - test_wrong_label: label "process_extreme" → error with valid labels in suggestion
  - test_missing_versions_topic: no "topic: 'versions'" → error
  - test_meta_yml_missing_field: meta.yml without "tools" key → warning
  - test_no_lint_flag: --no-lint skips quick_lint entirely
  All tests use temp directory fixtures with minimal valid/invalid file content.

docs.title: Prompt 18 — PyPI Packaging & README
docs.text:
Goal: Make the tool installable and document it properly.

Prepare code-to-module for distribution.

1. Create README.md with:
   - Project description and the gap it fills vs nf-core modules create
   - Quick install: pip install code-to-module
   - Usage examples showing all input types and container strategies:

     # Interactive — presents container options, you choose
     code-to-module script.py --outdir modules/
     code-to-module https://github.com/lab/tool --outdir modules/

     # Non-interactive — use default container strategy silently
     code-to-module script.py --outdir modules/ --no-interaction

     # Explicit container strategy
     code-to-module script.py --outdir modules/ --container biocontainers
     code-to-module script.py --outdir modules/ --container dockerfile
     code-to-module script.py --outdir modules/ --container generate
     code-to-module script.py --outdir modules/ --container stub

     # Inspect available container options without generating
     code-to-module containers https://github.com/lab/tool

     # Check for nf-core standards updates
     code-to-module update-standards
     code-to-module update-standards --check-only   # check without downloading

     # Skip staleness check in CI
     code-to-module convert script.py --no-update-check --no-interaction

   - Container strategy table:
     | Flag value      | Behaviour                                      |
     |-----------------|------------------------------------------------|
     | dockerfile      | Use Dockerfile found in repo                   |
     | biocontainers   | Use BioContainers (tool must be in Bioconda)   |
     | generate        | Generate Dockerfile from env/requirements file |
     | stub            | Insert TODO placeholders, fill in manually     |
     | (omitted)       | Interactive menu, or default if non-TTY        |

   - Complexity tier table (Tier 1-5 descriptions)
   - Default container selection logic (Tier 1–2 → BioContainers first;
     Tier 3–5 → Dockerfile/generated first)
   - Output file descriptions
   - "How it works" section: ingest → assess → discover containers →
     select container → infer → generate
   - Requirements and prerequisites section
   - Contributing section mentioning nf-core standards

2. Update pyproject.toml:
   - Add classifiers (Development Status, Topic :: Scientific/Engineering :: Bio-Informatics)
   - Add [project.urls] for Homepage, Issues, Documentation
   - Configure ruff linting rules (select E, F, I, UP; ignore E501)
   - Configure mypy strict mode
   - Verify both entry points are present:
       [project.scripts]
       code-to-module = "code_to_module.cli:main"
       validate-module = "code_to_module.validate_cli:main"
   - Add package data declaration so standards/data/ ships with the package:
       [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
       packages = ["src/code_to_module"]
       # hatchling includes all non-.py files by default; verify with:
       # pip install -e . && python -c "from code_to_module.standards import get_standards; print(get_standards().schema_version)"

   Runtime dependencies (add to [project] dependencies):
     "nf-core>=2.14"          ← minimum version that exposes --json flag on modules lint
     "anthropic>=0.28"        ← minimum version with AsyncAnthropic client
     "httpx>=0.27"            ← used by container discovery and bioconda checks
     "jinja2>=3.1"
     "pydantic>=2.0"
     "rich>=13.0"
     "click>=8.1"
     "gitpython>=3.1"         ← used by ingest for git tag version detection

   Note on the nf-core/tools integration boundary: code-to-module deliberately
   calls nf-core/tools as a subprocess (via "nf-core modules lint --json") rather
   than importing its internal Python API. Reasons:
     - nf-core/tools has no stable public Python API — internal classes are
       refactored frequently without versioned guarantees
     - The --json flag output IS a deliberate interface and is more stable
     - Subprocess isolation means a nf-core/tools crash or import error cannot
       take down code-to-module

   In validate.py and cli.py, whenever shelling out to nf-core:
     - Wrap subprocess.run() in a try/except FileNotFoundError
     - If nf-core is not on PATH, print a clear error:
         "nf-core tools not found. Install with: pip install nf-core>=2.14"
       and return an empty TestReport with a single NfTestFailure describing
       the missing dependency rather than crashing
     - Check the version at startup once:
         result = subprocess.run(["nf-core", "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True)
         parse the version string and warn if < 2.14

3. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml:
   - Triggers: push to main, pull_request
   - Jobs: lint (ruff check .), typecheck (mypy src/), test (pytest tests/ -v --tb=short)
   - Python versions matrix: 3.10, 3.11, 3.12
   - Cache pip dependencies

4. Create .devcontainer/devcontainer.json and .devcontainer/Dockerfile:

   .devcontainer/devcontainer.json:
   {
     "name": "code-to-module",
     "build": { "dockerfile": "Dockerfile" },
     "features": {
       "ghcr.io/devcontainers/features/node:1": { "version": "lts" },
       "ghcr.io/devcontainers/features/docker-in-docker:2": {}
     },
     "postCreateCommand": "pip install -e '.[dev]' && npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code",
     "customizations": {
       "vscode": {
         "extensions": [
           "ms-python.python",
           "ms-python.vscode-pylance",
           "charliermarsh.ruff",
           "nf-core.nf-core-extensionpack"
         ],
         "settings": {
           "python.defaultInterpreterPath": "/usr/local/bin/python",
           "editor.formatOnSave": true,
           "ruff.enable": true
         }
       }
     },
     "remoteEnv": {
       "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY": "${localEnv:ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}"
     }
   }

   .devcontainer/Dockerfile:
   FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/python:3.11

   # nf-core and nf-test
   RUN pip install --no-cache-dir "nf-core>=2.14"
   RUN curl -fsSL https://get.nf-test.com | bash && mv nf-test /usr/local/bin/

   # Java runtime required by nf-test
   RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
       default-jre-headless \
       && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

   # Nextflow (needed for integration tests)
   RUN curl -s https://get.nextflow.io | bash && mv nextflow /usr/local/bin/

   Notes:
   - Docker-in-Docker feature mounts the host Docker socket so container
     discovery tests that need docker can run inside the devcontainer.
   - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is forwarded from the host environment — never
     hardcode it in the Dockerfile or devcontainer.json.
   - Node.js is installed via the devcontainers feature so Claude Code
     (npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code) is available inside the container.
   - Java is required by nf-test; the headless JRE is sufficient.
   - nf-core is pinned to >=2.14 to match pyproject.toml's minimum.
   - The postCreateCommand runs after the container is built and the repo
     is mounted — it installs the project in editable mode with dev extras.

   Verify the devcontainer works by opening the repo in VSCode and selecting
   "Reopen in Container". After the build completes:
   python -c "from code_to_module.standards import get_standards; print(get_standards().schema_version)"
   nf-core --version
   nf-test --version
   claude --version
   echo "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:+set}${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-NOT SET}"

5. Create CHANGELOG.md starting with [0.1.0] section.

6. Run the full test suite: pytest tests/ -v
   Run linting: ruff check src/ tests/
   Fix any issues found.

7. Do dry-run tests against all three fixture repos:
   python -m code_to_module.cli convert tests/fixtures/repo_with_dockerfile/ --dry-run --no-interaction
   python -m code_to_module.cli convert tests/fixtures/repo_with_envyml/ --dry-run --no-interaction
   python -m code_to_module.cli convert tests/fixtures/repo_no_container/ --dry-run --no-interaction
   All should reach the "would generate" step without errors, each showing a different
   container strategy in the dry-run output.

docs.title: Prompt 19 — Programmatic Python API
docs.text:
Goal: Expose code-to-module as an importable Python library so that other tools in your ecosystem — a future web UI, CI pipeline scripts, or companion tools like git-to-module or snakemake-to-nextflow — can call it without shelling out to the CLI. This also decouples the display layer (Rich) from the logic layer, which is good architecture regardless of downstream consumers.

Why not import nf-core/tools directly here? The API in this prompt wraps code-to-module's own internals. Where code-to-module itself calls nf-core/tools, it does so via subprocess (see Prompt 18 note). This API layer does not add a new nf-core/tools dependency — it just makes code-to-module's existing functionality accessible to Python callers without going through the CLI.

Refactor so the CLI is a thin display layer over a clean Python API.

1. Create src/code_to_module/api.py:

from code_to_module.ingest import ingest
from code_to_module.assess import assess
from code_to_module.container import discover_sync, select
from code_to_module.infer import infer_all_sync
from code_to_module.test_gen import generate_test_spec
from code_to_module.generate import generate
from code_to_module.standards import get_standards
from code_to_module.models import ModuleSpec, CodeSource, ContainerOption

def convert(
    source: str,
    outdir: str | Path,
    tier_override: int | None = None,
    container: str | None = None,
    functionalities: str | None = None,   ← comma-separated names, or None for all
    dry_run: bool = False,
) -> dict:
    """
    Returns dict with keys:
    - 'success': bool
    - 'modules': list[dict]  ← one entry per generated module, each with:
        - 'functionality_name': str
        - 'process_name': str
        - 'tier': int
        - 'confidence': float
        - 'container_source': str
        - 'container_docker': str
        - 'test_data_strategies': dict[str, str]  ← {channel_name: strategy}
        - 'needs_derivation': bool
        - 'files_created': list[str]
        - 'warnings': list[str]
        - 'module_spec': dict
    - 'functionalities_found': list[str]   ← all detected names (before selection)
    - 'functionalities_selected': list[str] ← names that were generated
    - 'detection_method': str
    - 'error': str | None
    """
    standards = get_standards()
    ...

2. This API must NOT print anything (no Rich output) — return structured data only.
   Pass a no-op Rich console to container.select() to suppress menu output.
   The CLI layer calls this API and handles all display.
   Refactor cli.py to call api.convert() and display the results with Rich.

3. Also export helper functions for callers that want to inspect before committing
   (useful for GUIs, CI reporting, or companion tools):

   def get_container_options(source: str, functionality: str | None = None) -> list[dict]:
     """Return all available container options for the named functionality (or the
     single/first functionality if None). Useful for GUIs, CI reports, or any
     caller that wants to inspect options before calling convert()."""
     code_source = ingest(source)
     discovery = discover(code_source)
     func = next(
         (f for f in discovery.functionalities if f.name == functionality),
         discovery.functionalities[0]
     )
     tier, _, _ = assess(func, code_source)
     container_discovery = discover_sync(code_source, func.name, tier)
     return [opt.model_dump() for opt in container_discovery.options]

   def get_functionalities(source: str) -> list[dict]:
     """Return all detected functionalities with their metadata.
     Useful for any caller that wants to present or filter the selection
     before calling convert() — a GUI, a CI script, or a companion tool."""
     code_source = ingest(source)
     result = discover(code_source)
     return [
         {
             "name": f.name,
             "display_name": f.display_name,
             "description": f.description,
             "detection_method": f.detection_method.value,
             "confidence": f.confidence,
             "inferred_inputs": f.inferred_inputs,
             "inferred_outputs": f.inferred_outputs,
             "pre_selected": f.pre_selected,
             "warnings": f.warnings,
         }
         for f in result.functionalities
     ]

4. Add to __init__.py:
   from code_to_module.api import convert, get_container_options, get_functionalities
   __all__ = ["convert", "get_container_options", "get_functionalities"]

   This makes code-to-module usable as:
     from code_to_module import convert
   by any Python tool that lists code-to-module as a dependency.

5. Add a "Python API" section to README.md with usage examples
   (distinct from the CLI usage section):

# Basic usage — all detected functionalities
from code_to_module import convert

result = convert(
    source="https://github.com/lab/custom-aligner",
    outdir="modules/",
)
if result['success']:
    for mod in result['modules']:
        print(f"  {mod['functionality_name']}: {mod['container_source']} (Tier {mod['tier']})")

# Inspect functionalities before generating
from code_to_module import get_functionalities

funcs = get_functionalities("https://github.com/lab/custom-aligner")
for f in funcs:
    print(f"  {'★' if f['pre_selected'] else '⚠'} {f['name']}: {f['description']}")

# Generate only specific functionalities
result = convert(
    source="https://github.com/lab/custom-aligner",
    outdir="modules/",
    functionalities="align,sort",
    container="biocontainers",
)

# Inspect container options for one functionality
from code_to_module import get_container_options

options = get_container_options("https://github.com/lab/custom-aligner", functionality="align")
for opt in options:
    print(f"  {'★' if opt['is_default'] else ' '} {opt['label']}: {opt['docker_url']}")

6. Tests in tests/test_api.py:
   - test_convert_returns_structured_dict: mocked internals → result has all required keys
   - test_convert_modules_count: two functionalities selected → 'modules' list has two entries
   - test_convert_no_rich_output: api.convert() produces no stdout (no Rich panels)
   - test_convert_dry_run: dry_run=True → files_created is empty list, success=True
   - test_convert_error_handling: ingest raises exception → result['success']=False,
     result['error'] contains the exception message
   - test_get_functionalities_fields: returns list with name, confidence, pre_selected keys
   - test_get_container_options_filtered: functionality= parameter returns options for that func only
   - test_functionalities_param_filters: functionalities="align" → only align module generated
   - test_container_param_overrides: container="stub" → all modules use stub container
   - test_cli_calls_api: verify cli.py calls api.convert() and does not duplicate logic
     (use ast.parse to check cli.py imports from api module)
   All calls to ingest, assess, infer, container, generate mocked.

docs.title: Prompt 20 — test Command (Validation)
docs.text:
Goal: Run nf-core lint and nf-test, capture structured output, classify every failure into Class A / B / C, and produce a TestReport the fix command can consume.

Entry point: This command is exposed via validate-module test (the validate-module entry point defined in validate_cli.py), NOT via code-to-module test. Keep validate.py free of any imports from the conversion pipeline modules.

Implement src/code_to_module/validate.py.

Export:
  def run_validation(module_path: Path, standards: Standards) -> TestReport

This function runs both validators as subprocesses, parses their output into
structured failure objects, and classifies each failure. It never attempts fixes.

--- PART 1: nf-core lint ---

Run: nf-core modules lint {module_path} --json
Parse the JSON output into LintFailure objects.
If JSON output is unavailable, fall back to parsing the human-readable output.

Classification rules (applied in order, first match wins):

  CLASS_A (rule-based fix available):
    code contains "MODULE_MISSING_VERSIONS_TOPIC"  → missing topic: 'versions'
    code contains "MODULE_MISSING_EXT_ARGS"        → missing task.ext.args pattern
    code contains "MODULE_CONTAINER_URL_FORMAT"    → wrong registry prefix
    code contains "MODULE_META_YML_MISSING_FIELD"  → missing required meta.yml field
    code contains "MODULE_EMIT_NAME_MISMATCH"      → emit name ≠ meta.yml channel name
    code contains "MODULE_CONDA_CHANNEL_ORDER"     → wrong conda channel order

  CLASS_B (LLM-assisted fix available):
    code contains "MODULE_INCORRECT_OUTPUT_PATTERN" → glob pattern probably wrong
    code contains "MODULE_LABEL_INAPPROPRIATE"      → process label may not match resource needs
    code contains "MODULE_DESCRIPTION_MISSING"      → channel description incomplete

  CLASS_C (human only):
    code contains "MODULE_TEMPLATE_OUTDATED"   → template version mismatch
    code contains "MODULE_TEST_DATA_MISSING"   → no suitable test data found
    anything not matched by A or B rules       → default to CLASS_C

  Unknown lint codes: classify as CLASS_C with a note that the code is unrecognised.

--- PART 2: nf-test ---

Run: nf-test test {module_path}/tests/main.nf.test --verbose
Capture stdout + stderr. Parse into NfTestFailure objects.

Identify test name from lines matching:
  "FAILED" or "✘" followed by the test name string.
Identify error_type from the failure message:
  "No such file"              → file_not_found
  "Snapshot" or "mismatch"    → snapshot_mismatch
  "Process failed"            → process_failed
  anything else               → unknown

Classification rules:

  CLASS_A:
    error_type == snapshot_mismatch AND the snapshot file exists
      → snapshot needs refresh (output is deterministic, just changed)

  CLASS_B:
    error_type == file_not_found AND the path looks like a glob pattern
      → output filename pattern probably wrong
    error_type == process_failed AND stderr contains "command not found"
      → container missing a required tool

  CLASS_C:
    error_type == file_not_found AND path is a test input file
      → test data missing (human must provide)
    error_type == process_failed AND stderr contains tool-specific error messages
      → biological/logic error in the module
    error_type == unknown
      → cannot classify, human must investigate

--- PART 3: Aggregate and return ---

Compute class_a_count, class_b_count, class_c_count.
Set lint_passed = len(lint_failures) == 0
Set nftest_passed = len(nftest_failures) == 0

Print a Rich validation report:

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Validation: modules/my_aligner                         │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  nf-core lint   ✗  3 failures                           │
  │  nf-test        ✗  1 failure                            │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  Class A (auto-fixable)    2   run: code-to-module fix  │
  │  Class B (LLM-assisted)    1   run: code-to-module fix  │
  │  Class C (human only)      1   see details below        │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Class C failures require manual intervention:
  ✗ [lint] MODULE_TEMPLATE_OUTDATED
    The module template is out of date with nf-core/tools.
    Fix: nf-core modules patch my_aligner

For Class C failures: always print the full error message and a suggested
manual remediation step. Never leave the user with just an error code.

--- PART 4: Wire to CLI ---

Add to cli.py:
  @main.command("test")
  @click.argument("module_path", type=click.Path(exists=True))
  @click.option("--lint-only", is_flag=True)
  @click.option("--nftest-only", is_flag=True)
  @click.option("--json-output", type=click.Path(), default=None,
                help="Write TestReport as JSON to this path")
  def test_cmd(module_path, lint_only, nftest_only, json_output):
      """Run lint and nf-test, classify all failures."""

If --json-output: write TestReport.model_dump() as JSON (useful for CI pipelines).

--- Fixture setup ---

Create tests/fixtures/modules/passing/ — a minimal but complete correct module:
  main.nf with correct topic channel, ext.args, container block
  meta.yml with all required fields
  tests/main.nf.test with one real test and one stub test

Create tests/fixtures/modules/failing_lint/ — same but with specific Class A and B errors:
  Remove topic: 'versions' from versions channel (→ CLASS_A lint failure)
  Use wrong container URL prefix (→ CLASS_A lint failure)
  Use vague channel description (→ CLASS_B lint failure)

Create tests/fixtures/modules/failing_nftest/ — module where nf-test will fail:
  tests/main.nf.test references a wrong output filename pattern (→ CLASS_B nftest failure)
  tests/main.nf.test.snap exists but is stale (→ CLASS_A nftest failure)

Tests in tests/test_validate.py:
  - test_passing_module: run_validation on passing/ fixture → all counts 0, both passed
  - test_lint_class_a_detection: failing_lint/ → class_a_count >= 2
  - test_lint_class_b_detection: failing_lint/ → class_b_count >= 1
  - test_nftest_snapshot_is_class_a: stale snapshot → NfTestFailure with CLASS_A
  - test_nftest_wrong_pattern_is_class_b: wrong output pattern → CLASS_B
  - test_class_c_default: unrecognised lint code → CLASS_C, message preserved
  - test_json_output: --json-output flag writes valid TestReport JSON
  Mock subprocess calls — never run real nf-core lint or nf-test in unit tests.
  Integration test (marked @pytest.mark.integration, skipped in CI):
    run against the passing/ fixture with real nf-core lint installed.

docs.title: Prompt 21 — fix Command
docs.text:
Goal: Given a TestReport, propose concrete fixes for Class A and Class B failures as unified diffs. Always show the diff before writing. Always re-validate after applying.

Entry point: Exposed via validate-module fix. Keep fix.py free of imports from the conversion pipeline (ingest, discover, assess, infer, container, generate).

Implement src/code_to_module/fix.py.

Export:
  def propose_fixes(
      report: TestReport,
      standards: Standards,
  ) -> list[ProposedFix]

  def apply_fix(fix: ProposedFix) -> bool
      """Write the fix to disk. Returns True on success. Only call after user approval."""

  def apply_approved_fixes(fixes: list[ProposedFix]) -> int
      """Apply all fixes with approved=True. Returns count of successfully written fixes."""

--- PART 1: Rule-based fixes (Class A) ---

Implement one rule function per Class A failure type.
Each returns a ProposedFix with fix_source=RULE.

Rule: missing topic channel tag
  Read main.nf, find the versions emit line.
  Old: `path "versions.yml", emit: versions`
  New: `path "versions.yml", emit: versions, topic: 'versions'`
  Generate unified diff. Set description: "Add topic: 'versions' to versions channel emit"

Rule: missing ext.args pattern
  Find the script: block in main.nf.
  If `task.ext.args` is absent, insert after the opening triple-quote:
  `def args = task.ext.args ?: ''`
  Generate unified diff. Set description: "Add task.ext.args pattern to script block"

Rule: wrong container URL prefix
  Find the container line. Replace docker.io/biocontainers/ with quay.io/biocontainers/
  Generate unified diff. Set description: "Fix container URL: use quay.io/biocontainers"

Rule: meta.yml missing required field
  Read meta.yml, identify which required field is absent (from standards.meta_yml_required_fields).
  Insert a stub entry with a TODO placeholder at the correct location.
  Generate unified diff. Set description: "Add missing meta.yml field: {field_name}"

Rule: emit name mismatch
  Find the mismatched emit name in main.nf and the canonical name in meta.yml.
  Replace the emit name in main.nf to match meta.yml.
  Generate unified diff. Set description: "Fix emit name: '{old}' → '{new}' to match meta.yml"

Rule: stale nf-test snapshot
  Delete the existing .snap file.
  Note in description: "Snapshot deleted — will be regenerated on next nf-test run"
  The "diff" for this fix is a deletion notice, not a text diff.
  Set fix_source=RULE.

--- PART 2: LLM-assisted fixes (Class B) ---

For each Class B failure, call the Anthropic API with a focused prompt.
Each LLM fix returns a ProposedFix with fix_source=LLM.

Use model claude-sonnet-4-20250514, max_tokens=500.
Keep prompts narrow and file-scoped — never send the entire module.

LLM fix: wrong output filename pattern
  System: "You are fixing an nf-core module. Return ONLY a unified diff, nothing else."
  User:
    "The nf-test for process {process_name} failed because the output file was not found.
     Expected pattern: {pattern_from_meta_yml}
     Actual nf-test error: {error_message}

     Script block from main.nf:
     {script_block}   ← extract only the script: ... """ block, not the full file

     The output glob pattern in main.nf is wrong. Provide a unified diff that fixes
     only the pattern: line in the output block of main.nf.
     Return ONLY the unified diff, starting with ---."

  Parse the response as a unified diff. If it doesn't parse cleanly:
    set ProposedFix.approved = False automatically
    add a note: "LLM returned unparseable diff — manual fix required"
    degrade to CLASS_C behaviour (explain and stop)

LLM fix: inappropriate process label
  System: "You are fixing an nf-core module. Return ONLY a unified diff, nothing else."
  User:
    "The process label for {process_name} is '{current_label}'.
     The lint check suggests this is inappropriate.
     Lint message: {lint_message}

     Valid labels and their resource limits:
     {standards.label_resources as formatted table}

     Based on the tool name and lint message, what is the correct label?
     Provide a unified diff that changes only the label: line in main.nf.
     Return ONLY the unified diff, starting with ---."

LLM fix: incomplete channel description
  User:
    "The channel '{channel_name}' in meta.yml has an incomplete description: '{current_desc}'
     The channel pattern is: '{pattern}'
     The tool is: {tool_name}

     Write a concise, accurate one-sentence description for this channel.
     Provide a unified diff that updates only the description: line in meta.yml.
     Return ONLY the unified diff, starting with ---."

--- PART 3: Presenting fixes to the user ---

In cli.py, the fix command:
1. Runs run_validation() to get a fresh TestReport (or loads --from-report JSON)
2. Calls propose_fixes() to get all ProposedFix objects
3. For each ProposedFix, display a Rich panel:

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Fix 1 of 4  [rule]  Missing topic channel tag              │
  │  File: modules/my_aligner/main.nf                           │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  --- main.nf                                                │
  │  +++ main.nf                                                │
  │  @@ -24,1 +24,1 @@                                          │
  │  -    path "versions.yml", emit: versions                   │
  │  +    path "versions.yml", emit: versions, topic: 'versions'│
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  Apply this fix? [Y/n/skip all LLM fixes]:                  │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  For LLM fixes: add a yellow banner in the panel:
  "⚡ LLM-proposed fix — review carefully before approving"

  Options at the prompt:
    Y / Enter  → approve this fix
    n          → skip this fix (leave failure unresolved)
    a          → approve all remaining fixes of the same class (A or B)
    s          → skip all remaining fixes of the same class
    q          → quit fix mode, apply only already-approved fixes

4. Call apply_approved_fixes(fixes)
5. Print summary: "Applied {n} fixes. Re-running validation..."
6. Call run_validation() again and print the new TestReport
7. If all failures resolved: print "✓ All fixable failures resolved"
   If failures remain:
     Print remaining failures grouped by class
     For Class C: print full explanation and suggested manual steps


Additional fix command interaction rules:

--- Fix ordering ---
Always present fixes in this order:
  1. Class A fixes (rule-based) — most likely to be correct, present first
  2. Class B fixes (LLM-assisted) — require more careful review
  3. Class C explanations — not fixable; shown at the end as informational

Within each class, present fixes in the order lint/nf-test reported them.

--- Fix dependency invalidation ---
Some fixes make subsequent fixes invalid. After applying each fix, check:
  - If a Class A "missing ext.args" fix was applied AND a Class B "wrong script block"
    fix is pending → invalidate the Class B fix (the script block changed).
    Mark the Class B ProposedFix as invalidated=True, skip it in the loop,
    print: "⚠ Fix 3 skipped — invalidated by Fix 1 (script block changed)"
  - If a snapshot deletion fix was applied, any subsequent snapshot-related
    nf-test failures are no longer meaningful — skip them.

Rule: a fix is invalidated if it targets the same file and overlapping line ranges
as an already-applied fix. Implement this check in apply_approved_fixes().

--- Single-fix and batch-approve transcripts ---

TRANSCRIPT: approving fixes one by one (default mode)

  $ validate-module fix modules/my_aligner

  Running validation...
  ✗ 3 failures: 2 Class A, 1 Class B

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Fix 1 of 3  [rule]  Missing topic channel tag                       │
  │  File: modules/my_aligner/main.nf  line 24                           │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  --- main.nf                                                         │
  │  +++ main.nf                                                         │
  │  @@ -24,1 +24,1 @@                                                   │
  │  -    path "versions.yml", emit: versions                            │
  │  +    path "versions.yml", emit: versions, topic: 'versions'         │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  Apply this fix? [Y/n/a/s/q] (Y=yes, a=approve all Class A, s=skip  │
  │  all Class A, q=quit):                                               │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  Y

  ✓ Fix 1 applied

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Fix 2 of 3  [rule]  Wrong container URL prefix                      │
  │  File: modules/my_aligner/main.nf  line 8                            │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  --- main.nf                                                         │
  │  +++ main.nf                                                         │
  │  @@ -8,1 +8,1 @@                                                     │
  │  -    container "docker.io/biocontainers/bwa:0.7.17--h5bf99c6_8"    │
  │  +    container "quay.io/biocontainers/bwa:0.7.17--h5bf99c6_8"      │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  Apply this fix? [Y/n/a/s/q]:                                        │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  a

  → Approving all remaining Class A fixes automatically.
  ✓ Fix 2 applied

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Fix 3 of 3  [LLM]  Wrong output filename pattern                   │
  │  File: modules/my_aligner/main.nf  line 31                           │
  │  ⚡ LLM-proposed fix — review carefully before approving             │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  --- main.nf                                                         │
  │  +++ main.nf                                                         │
  │  @@ -31,1 +31,1 @@                                                   │
  │  -    path "*.bam"                                                   │
  │  +    path "${prefix}.bam"                                           │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  Apply this fix? [Y/n/a/s/q]:                                        │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  Y

  ✓ Fix 3 applied

  Applied 3 fixes. Re-running validation...

  ✓ All fixable failures resolved

TRANSCRIPT: --yes flag (auto-approve Class A, still prompt for Class B)

  $ validate-module fix modules/my_aligner --yes

  Running validation...
  ✗ 3 failures: 2 Class A, 1 Class B

  Auto-approving Class A fixes...
  ✓ Fix 1 applied: Missing topic channel tag
  ✓ Fix 2 applied: Wrong container URL prefix

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Fix 3 of 3  [LLM]  Wrong output filename pattern                   │
  │  ⚡ LLM-proposed fix — --yes does not auto-approve LLM fixes         │
  │  ...diff shown...                                                    │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  Apply this fix? [Y/n/q]:                                            │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSCRIPT: remaining Class C failure after applying fixes

  Applied 2 fixes. Re-running validation...

  ✗ 1 failure remains (Class C — manual fix required):

  ╭─ CLASS C: Custom output format not matching meta.yml ─────────────────╮
  │  Process: MY_ALIGNER                                                  │
  │  Failure: nf-test output file 'result.custom' not matched by any      │
  │           pattern in meta.yml                                         │
  │                                                                       │
  │  This cannot be fixed automatically. Suggested steps:                 │
  │    1. Open main.nf and find the line producing 'result.custom'        │
  │    2. Open meta.yml and add an output entry with the correct pattern  │
  │    3. Re-run: validate-module test modules/my_aligner                    │
  │                                                                       │
  │  If the file is intentional and nf-core reviewers accept it, you      │
  │  may need to add a custom nf-test assertion rather than relying on    │
  │  the auto-generated pattern check.                                    │
  ╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Notes:
- 'a' (approve all of class) applies to the current class only.
  After approving all Class A with 'a', Class B fixes still prompt individually.
- 'q' applies already-approved fixes and exits — does NOT discard approved fixes.
- The re-validation always runs unless --no-revalidate is passed.
- Class C panels are always shown at the end, even if no fixes were applied.
  They explain what to do manually. They do not count as "failures resolved".


--- CLI command signature ---
  @main.command("fix")
  @click.argument("module_path", type=click.Path(exists=True))
  @click.option("--from-report", type=click.Path(), default=None,
                help="Load existing TestReport JSON instead of re-running validation")
  @click.option("--class-a-only", is_flag=True, help="Only propose rule-based fixes")
  @click.option("--yes", "-y", is_flag=True, help="Auto-approve all Class A fixes (not B)")
  @click.option("--no-revalidate", is_flag=True, help="Skip re-validation after applying")
  def fix_cmd(module_path, from_report, class_a_only, yes, no_revalidate):
      """Propose and apply fixes for lint and nf-test failures."""

The --yes flag auto-approves ONLY Class A (rule-based) fixes.
It never auto-approves Class B (LLM) fixes — those always require human review.

--- Fixture setup ---
Reuse tests/fixtures/modules/failing_lint/ and failing_nftest/ from Prompt 20.

Tests in tests/test_fix.py:
  - test_rule_missing_topic: LintFailure with correct code → ProposedFix with RULE source,
    unified diff contains "topic: 'versions'"
  - test_rule_wrong_container_prefix: ProposedFix diff replaces docker.io with quay.io
  - test_rule_stale_snapshot: NfTestFailure snapshot_mismatch → ProposedFix describes deletion
  - test_llm_fix_output_pattern: mock Anthropic API returning valid diff →
    ProposedFix with LLM source, diff parses cleanly
  - test_llm_fix_unparseable_response: mock API returning prose instead of diff →
    ProposedFix.approved = False, failure degraded to CLASS_C note
  - test_apply_fix_writes_file: apply_fix() on a rule fix → file contents match expected
  - test_apply_fix_does_not_write_unapproved: approved=False → file unchanged
  - test_yes_flag_approves_only_class_a: --yes auto-approves CLASS_A, CLASS_B still prompted
  - test_revalidation_after_fix: after applying fixes, run_validation called again
  Mock all subprocess (nf-core lint, nf-test) and Anthropic API calls.
  Use tmp_path for all file writes — never modify real fixtures.

docs.title: Prompt 22 — review Command
docs.text:
Goal: Static analysis of a generated module against nf-core style conventions, without running any external tools. Produces a ReviewReport that helps first-time contributors understand what nf-core reviewers will look for.

Entry point: Exposed via validate-module review. Keep review.py free of imports from the conversion pipeline (ingest, discover, assess, infer, container, generate).

Implement src/code_to_module/review.py.

Export:
  def review_module(module_path: Path, standards: Standards) -> ReviewReport

This function reads module files and applies a set of static analysis checks.
It never runs subprocesses. It produces ReviewItems with severity levels.

--- CHECK CATEGORY: channel_naming ---

Read main.nf. For each output channel emit name:

  ERROR if emit name is a generic name with no meaning:
    names like: output, result, out, file, data, files, results
    message: "Output channel '{name}' is too generic — use a format-specific name like 'bam', 'vcf', 'report'"

  WARNING if input channel name doesn't follow the tuple convention:
    first input should always be named 'meta' (type: map)
    message: "First input channel should be named 'meta' — found '{name}'"

  INFO if a channel name could be more descriptive:
    single-letter names or abbreviations that aren't standard format names
    message: "Consider a more descriptive channel name for '{name}'"

--- CHECK CATEGORY: process_label ---

Read the label: directive from main.nf.
Read the container line to identify the tool.
Apply heuristics:

  ERROR if label is process_single but tool is known to be memory-intensive:
    known heavy tools: gatk, star, hisat2, cellranger, bismark
    message: "'{tool}' typically requires more resources than process_single"

  WARNING if label is process_high_memory and tool is a simple utility:
    known light tools: samtools view, bedtools intersect, bcftools view
    message: "'{tool}' is typically lightweight — consider process_single or process_medium"

  INFO if process label cannot be verified:
    tool name not recognised
    message: "Could not verify label appropriateness for '{tool}' — review manually"

--- CHECK CATEGORY: ext_args ---

Read the script: block.

  ERROR if task.ext.args is absent:
    message: "Module must use 'task.ext.args' — hardcoded parameters are not accepted"

  WARNING if task.ext.prefix is absent and the module produces named output files:
    message: "Consider using 'task.ext.prefix ?: meta.id' for output file naming"

  INFO if extra arguments are hardcoded in the script alongside ext.args:
    e.g. `tool $args --threads $task.cpus --specific-flag`
    message: "Hardcoded flag '--specific-flag' should be movable to ext.args"

--- CHECK CATEGORY: meta_yml ---

Read meta.yml as YAML. Apply checks from standards.meta_yml_required_fields.

  ERROR for each missing required top-level field
  ERROR for each tool entry missing homepage or documentation URL
  ERROR if any input/output channel in meta.yml has no corresponding emit in main.nf
    (or vice versa — channel in main.nf not documented in meta.yml)

  WARNING if EDAM ontology term is missing for a channel whose format is in standards.edam_for
    message: "Channel '{name}' has format {fmt} — add ontology: [{edam_uri}]"

  WARNING if tool description is identical to the tool name (placeholder not filled in)
    message: "Tool description appears to be a placeholder — add a real description"

  INFO if keywords list has fewer than 3 entries
    message: "Add more keywords to improve discoverability"

--- CHECK CATEGORY: versions ---

Read main.nf script block.

  ERROR if no `versions.yml` is written in the script:
    message: "Module must capture software version in versions.yml"

  WARNING if the version capture command uses a fragile pattern:
    e.g. `{tool} --version` without error handling (should use `|| true` or `2>&1`)
    message: "Version capture may fail if tool exits non-zero — add '|| true'"

  INFO if version capture uses a non-standard format:
    expected: `echo "{tool_name}:\$(tool --version 2>&1 | head -1)" >> versions.yml`
    message: "Non-standard version capture pattern — check nf-core module template"

--- CHECK CATEGORY: conda ---

Read environment.yml.

  ERROR if conda channels are not in the correct order per standards.conda_channels:
    message: "Conda channels must be ordered: {expected_order}"

  WARNING if a dependency has no pinned version:
    e.g. `- samtools` instead of `- samtools=1.18`
    message: "Pin dependency versions in environment.yml: '{pkg}' has no version"

  INFO if the environment name doesn't match the tool name:
    message: "Convention: environment name should match tool name — found '{env_name}'"

--- PART 2: Aggregate and display ---

After all checks, compute counts and set submission_ready = (error_count == 0).

Print a Rich review report:

  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Review: modules/my_aligner                                │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  ✗  3 errors (must fix before submission)                  │
  │  ⚠  2 warnings (should fix)                               │
  │  ℹ  4 suggestions                                          │
  │                                                            │
  │  Submission ready: NO                                      │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Then list each item grouped by category, with severity icon:
    ✗ [meta_yml]   Tool entry missing 'homepage' URL
    ✗ [ext_args]   Module must use 'task.ext.args'
    ✗ [meta_yml]   Output channel 'bam' in main.nf not documented in meta.yml
    ⚠ [meta_yml]   Channel 'reads' missing EDAM ontology term — add format_1930
    ⚠ [conda]      Pin dependency versions: 'samtools' has no version
    ℹ [channel_naming]  Consider a more descriptive name for channel 'out'
    ...

  If submission_ready is True:
    Print a green "✓ No blocking issues — module looks ready for submission"
    Print reminder: "Run 'nf-core modules lint' and 'nf-test' as final checks"

--- CLI command ---
  @main.command("review")
  @click.argument("module_path", type=click.Path(exists=True))
  @click.option("--errors-only", is_flag=True, help="Show only ERROR severity items")
  @click.option("--json-output", type=click.Path(), default=None)
  def review_cmd(module_path, errors_only, json_output):
      """Static analysis of a module against nf-core style conventions."""

--- Fixture setup ---

Create tests/fixtures/modules/needs_review/ — a module that passes lint and nf-test
but has multiple style issues:
  - Generic output channel name "output"
  - No EDAM ontology terms in meta.yml
  - Unpinned conda dependency
  - No task.ext.prefix usage despite named outputs
  - Version capture without error handling

Tests in tests/test_review.py:
  - test_passing_module_is_submission_ready: passing/ fixture → submission_ready=True,
    error_count=0
  - test_generic_channel_name_is_error: "output" emit name → ReviewItem ERROR channel_naming
  - test_missing_edam_ontology_is_warning: known format without ontology → WARNING meta_yml
  - test_missing_homepage_is_error: tool entry without homepage → ERROR meta_yml
  - test_unpinned_dependency_is_warning: `- samtools` → WARNING conda
  - test_missing_ext_args_is_error: no task.ext.args in script → ERROR ext_args
  - test_heavy_tool_wrong_label: gatk with process_single → ERROR process_label
  - test_channel_not_in_meta_yml_is_error: emit name in main.nf not in meta.yml → ERROR
  - test_errors_only_flag: errors_only=True → INFO and WARNING items filtered from output
  - test_json_output: --json-output flag writes valid ReviewReport JSON
  All tests read from fixture files — no subprocess calls, no mocking needed.

docs.title: Prompt 23 — End-to-End Chain Test
docs.text:
Goal: Automated test that runs the full convert → test → fix → review pipeline on a single fixture module without making any LLM calls. Catches cross-module regressions that isolated unit tests miss — the most likely place for silent failures to hide once the codebase is stable.

No new source files. This prompt adds tests/test_chain.py and one new fixture only.

Implement tests/test_chain.py.

This test exercises the full pipeline — convert, test, fix, review — as a chain
on a single synthetic fixture, with the LLM inference step replaced by a
pre-baked ModuleSpec.

--- FIXTURE ---

Create tests/fixtures/modules/chain_input/ — a minimal synthetic tool repo:
  chain_input/
    setup.py          (entry_points: console_scripts = ["chaintool = chaintool:main"])
    chaintool.py      (argparse: --input FILE, --output FILE, --threads INT)
    README.md         (one-line: "chaintool: a synthetic test tool")

This is the tool that convert will "discover". No real CLI run is needed —
discovery reads the source files, which are deterministic.

Create tests/fixtures/specs/chaintool_spec.json — a pre-baked ModuleSpec JSON
that infer.py would produce for chaintool:
  {
    "tool_name": "chaintool",
    "process_name": "CHAINTOOL",
    "container_docker": "quay.io/biocontainers/chaintool:1.0.0--h1234567_0",
    "container_singularity": "https://depot.galaxyproject.org/singularity/chaintool:1.0.0--h1234567_0",
    "label": "process_single",
    "inputs": [
      {"name": "meta",  "type": "val",  "qualifier": "map",  "optional": false},
      {"name": "input", "type": "path", "qualifier": "file", "optional": false}
    ],
    "outputs": [
      {"name": "meta",     "type": "val",  "qualifier": "map"},
      {"name": "output",   "type": "path", "qualifier": "*.txt"},
      {"name": "versions", "type": "path", "qualifier": "versions.yml"}
    ],
    "script_template": "chaintool $args \\\n    --input $input \\\n    --output ${prefix}.txt",
    "version_command": "chaintool --version 2>&1 | head -1",
    "conda_packages": ["bioconda::chaintool=1.0.0"]
  }

--- SEAM ---

Add an optional parameter to cli.py's convert() function (not the Click command):
  def convert(source, outdir, ..., _inject_spec: ModuleSpec | None = None)

If _inject_spec is not None, skip the infer() call and use it directly.
This is the only change to production code this prompt requires.
The Click command never passes _inject_spec (it stays None in normal usage).

--- TESTS ---

def test_chain_discovery(tmp_path):
    """Discovery finds chaintool entry point from fixture repo."""
    functionalities = discover(Path("tests/fixtures/modules/chain_input"))
    assert len(functionalities) >= 1
    assert any(f.name == "chaintool" for f in functionalities)

def test_chain_convert_produces_module(tmp_path):
    """convert() with injected spec writes all required module files."""
    spec = ModuleSpec.model_validate_json(
        Path("tests/fixtures/specs/chaintool_spec.json").read_text()
    )
    convert(
        source=Path("tests/fixtures/modules/chain_input"),
        outdir=tmp_path,
        no_interaction=True,
        _inject_spec=spec,
    )
    module_dir = tmp_path / "chaintool"
    assert (module_dir / "main.nf").exists()
    assert (module_dir / "meta.yml").exists()
    assert (module_dir / "environment.yml").exists()
    assert (module_dir / "tests" / "main.nf.test").exists()

def test_chain_review_after_convert(tmp_path):
    """review_module() on a convert() output returns a ReviewReport."""
    spec = ModuleSpec.model_validate_json(
        Path("tests/fixtures/specs/chaintool_spec.json").read_text()
    )
    convert(
        source=Path("tests/fixtures/modules/chain_input"),
        outdir=tmp_path,
        no_interaction=True,
        _inject_spec=spec,
    )
    standards = get_standards()
    report = review_module(tmp_path / "chaintool", standards)
    assert isinstance(report, ReviewReport)
    # Generated module from a clean spec should have zero errors
    assert report.error_count == 0

def test_chain_main_nf_invariants(tmp_path):
    """Generated main.nf satisfies structural invariants regardless of LLM."""
    spec = ModuleSpec.model_validate_json(
        Path("tests/fixtures/specs/chaintool_spec.json").read_text()
    )
    convert(
        source=Path("tests/fixtures/modules/chain_input"),
        outdir=tmp_path,
        no_interaction=True,
        _inject_spec=spec,
    )
    main_nf = (tmp_path / "chaintool" / "main.nf").read_text()
    assert "process CHAINTOOL" in main_nf
    assert "tuple val(meta)" in main_nf          # meta is first input
    assert "emit: versions" in main_nf            # versions channel present
    assert main_nf.count("emit: versions") == 1  # no duplicate
    assert "TODO" not in main_nf                  # no placeholders
    assert "task.ext.args" in main_nf             # ext.args pattern present

No subprocess calls. No mocking beyond the _inject_spec seam. All assertions
read from generated files on disk.

docs.title: Prompt 24 — Import Boundary Enforcement
docs.text:
Goal: Permanent, zero-maintenance protection against the most likely architectural drift: validate_cli.py, review.py, fix.py, or standards/ accidentally importing from the conversion pipeline. Two dozen lines of stdlib code; runs in milliseconds; catches violations before they reach production.

No new source files. This prompt adds tests/test_import_boundaries.py only.

Implement tests/test_import_boundaries.py.

Use Python's ast module to parse every .py file under src/code_to_module/
and collect its import statements. Assert that the following modules never
import from the conversion pipeline.

PROTECTED_MODULES — must not import from CONVERSION_PIPELINE:
  src/code_to_module/validate_cli.py
  src/code_to_module/validate.py
  src/code_to_module/fix.py
  src/code_to_module/review.py
  src/code_to_module/standards/ (all files recursively)

CONVERSION_PIPELINE — must not be imported by protected modules:
  code_to_module.ingest
  code_to_module.discover
  code_to_module.assess
  code_to_module.infer
  code_to_module.container
  code_to_module.generate

Implementation:

def get_imports(path: Path) -> set[str]:
    """Return all module names imported by the file at path."""
    tree = ast.parse(path.read_text())
    imports = set()
    for node in ast.walk(tree):
        if isinstance(node, ast.Import):
            for alias in node.names:
                imports.add(alias.name)
        elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):
            if node.module:
                imports.add(node.module)
    return imports

@pytest.mark.parametrize("protected_file", PROTECTED_FILES)
def test_no_conversion_pipeline_imports(protected_file):
    imports = get_imports(protected_file)
    violations = imports & CONVERSION_PIPELINE_MODULES
    assert not violations, (
        f"{protected_file.name} imports from conversion pipeline: {violations}\n"
        "These modules must remain independent of the conversion pipeline."
    )

Also add a complementary test:

def test_standards_subpackage_is_self_contained():
    """standards/ must not import from any other code_to_module submodule
    except other standards/ files. It is a read-only data layer."""
    standards_files = list(Path("src/code_to_module/standards").rglob("*.py"))
    allowed_prefix = "code_to_module.standards"
    for f in standards_files:
        imports = get_imports(f)
        violations = {
            imp for imp in imports
            if imp.startswith("code_to_module") and not imp.startswith(allowed_prefix)
        }
        assert not violations, (
            f"{f.name} imports from outside standards/: {violations}"
        )

No fixtures needed. No subprocess calls. Runs entirely on source files.

docs.title: Prompt 25 — Discovery Robustness Suite
docs.text:
Goal: Parametrized integration tests that run the discovery pipeline against real GitHub repos with known CLI shapes, catching parser edge cases that synthetic fixtures miss. Repos are cloned once into tests/fixtures/repos/ and cached — subsequent runs use the local copy.

No new source files. This prompt adds tests/test_discovery_robustness.py and a tests/conftest.py fixture for repo caching.

Implement tests/test_discovery_robustness.py and extend tests/conftest.py.

--- REFERENCE REPOS ---

Four repos covering the major CLI shapes code-to-module must handle:

DISCOVERY_TARGETS = [
    {
        "id": "celltypist",
        "url": "https://github.com/Teichlab/celltypist",
        "shape": "console_scripts",        # setup.py entry_points
        "expected_names": ["celltypist"],
        "min_functionalities": 1,
        "max_functionalities": 2,          # annotate + train at most
    },
    {
        "id": "fastqc_wrapper",
        "url": "https://github.com/nf-core/fastqc",  # use a known simple CLI
        "shape": "argparse_flat",
        "expected_names": ["fastqc"],
        "min_functionalities": 1,
        "max_functionalities": 1,
    },
    {
        "id": "multiqc",
        "url": "https://github.com/MultiQC/MultiQC",
        "shape": "click_flat",             # Click single-command with many options
        "expected_names": ["multiqc"],
        "min_functionalities": 1,
        "max_functionalities": 1,
    },
    {
        "id": "pyfastx",
        "url": "https://github.com/lmdu/pyfastx",
        "shape": "argparse_subcommands",   # argparse with subparsers
        "min_functionalities": 2,
        "max_functionalities": 6,
    },
]

Replace any repo that returns 0 results consistently — the point is to cover
the four shapes, not these exact tools. Document the swap in a comment.

--- CONFTEST FIXTURE ---

In tests/conftest.py, add a session-scoped fixture:

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def repo_cache(tmp_path_factory):
    """Clone each repo once per test session into a shared cache directory."""
    cache = tmp_path_factory.mktemp("repos")
    clones = {}
    for target in DISCOVERY_TARGETS:
        dest = cache / target["id"]
        subprocess.run(
            ["git", "clone", "--depth=1", target["url"], str(dest)],
            check=True, capture_output=True
        )
        clones[target["id"]] = dest
    return clones

Mark these tests with @pytest.mark.network so they can be skipped in offline CI:
  pytest -m "not network"   # skips this suite
  pytest -m network         # runs only this suite

--- TESTS ---

@pytest.mark.network
@pytest.mark.parametrize("target", DISCOVERY_TARGETS, ids=[t["id"] for t in DISCOVERY_TARGETS])
def test_discovery_returns_functionalities(target, repo_cache):
    """Discovery finds at least one functionality for every supported CLI shape."""
    repo_path = repo_cache[target["id"]]
    functionalities = discover(repo_path)
    assert len(functionalities) >= target["min_functionalities"], (
        f"{target['id']}: expected >= {target['min_functionalities']} functionalities, "
        f"got {len(functionalities)}"
    )
    assert len(functionalities) <= target["max_functionalities"], (
        f"{target['id']}: expected <= {target['max_functionalities']} functionalities, "
        f"got {len(functionalities)} — LLM may have over-split"
    )

@pytest.mark.network
@pytest.mark.parametrize("target", DISCOVERY_TARGETS, ids=[t["id"] for t in DISCOVERY_TARGETS])
def test_discovery_no_llm_call(target, repo_cache, mocker):
    """Rule-based detection handles all four shapes without calling the LLM."""
    mock_infer = mocker.patch("code_to_module.infer.infer_module_spec")
    repo_path = repo_cache[target["id"]]
    discover(repo_path)
    mock_infer.assert_not_called()

@pytest.mark.network
@pytest.mark.parametrize("target", DISCOVERY_TARGETS, ids=[t["id"] for t in DISCOVERY_TARGETS])
def test_discovery_process_names_are_valid(target, repo_cache):
    """All returned process names are non-empty uppercase strings."""
    repo_path = repo_cache[target["id"]]
    functionalities = discover(repo_path)
    for f in functionalities:
        assert f.process_name, f"{target['id']}: empty process name"
        assert f.process_name == f.process_name.upper(), (
            f"{target['id']}: process name not uppercase: {f.process_name}"
        )
        assert f.confidence > 0.0, (
            f"{target['id']}: zero confidence for {f.process_name}"
        )

@pytest.mark.network
def test_celltypist_finds_console_scripts_entry_point(repo_cache):
    """Celltypist-specific: console_scripts detector fires before AST detectors."""
    repo_path = repo_cache["celltypist"]
    functionalities = discover(repo_path)
    names = [f.name for f in functionalities]
    assert "celltypist" in names, (
        "console_scripts detector missed the celltypist entry point — "
        "check _detect_console_scripts in discover.py"
    )

Add network to pytest markers in pyproject.toml:
  [tool.pytest.ini_options]
  markers = [
      "integration: slow integration tests",
      "network: tests that clone real GitHub repos",
      "llm: tests that make real Anthropic API calls",
  ]

docs.title: Prompt 26 — LLM Contract Tests
docs.text:
Goal: Opt-in tests (skipped automatically if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is absent) that make real API calls with a minimal synthetic tool and assert structural invariants on the raw LLM output. These verify that infer.py's post-processing guards are correct — both that the LLM produces invariants naturally, and that the guards catch it when it doesn't.

No new source files. This prompt adds tests/test_llm_contract.py only.

Implement tests/test_llm_contract.py.

All tests in this file are marked @pytest.mark.llm and are skipped automatically
if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set in the environment.

import os
import pytest

pytestmark = pytest.mark.llm

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def require_api_key():
    if not os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"):
        pytest.skip("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set — skipping LLM contract tests")

--- MINIMAL TOOL FIXTURE ---

Use tests/fixtures/modules/chain_input/ from Prompt 23.
This is small enough that the LLM call is cheap and fast.
The fixture has known-correct CLI shape: argparse flat, one input, one output.

--- TESTS ---

def test_infer_returns_module_spec(tmp_path):
    """infer() returns a valid ModuleSpec for the chaintool fixture."""
    source = ingest(Path("tests/fixtures/modules/chain_input"))
    functionalities = discover(source)
    assert functionalities, "Discovery returned nothing — chain test prerequisite failed"
    spec = infer(source, functionalities[0])
    assert isinstance(spec, ModuleSpec)

def test_meta_is_first_input(tmp_path):
    """LLM output always has meta as the first input channel after post-processing."""
    source = ingest(Path("tests/fixtures/modules/chain_input"))
    functionalities = discover(source)
    spec = infer(source, functionalities[0])
    assert spec.inputs, "No inputs in spec"
    assert spec.inputs[0].name == "meta", (
        f"First input is '{spec.inputs[0].name}', expected 'meta' — "
        "check meta enforcement post-processing in infer.py"
    )

def test_no_duplicate_versions_emit(tmp_path):
    """Generated main.nf has exactly one versions emit."""
    source = ingest(Path("tests/fixtures/modules/chain_input"))
    functionalities = discover(source)
    spec = infer(source, functionalities[0])
    main_nf = render_module(spec)  # render without writing to disk
    count = main_nf.count("emit: versions")
    assert count == 1, (
        f"Found {count} versions emits in generated main.nf — "
        "check duplicate-versions guard in generate.py"
    )

def test_no_todo_in_script_block(tmp_path):
    """Generated script block contains no TODO placeholders."""
    source = ingest(Path("tests/fixtures/modules/chain_input"))
    functionalities = discover(source)
    spec = infer(source, functionalities[0])
    main_nf = render_module(spec)
    assert "TODO" not in main_nf, (
        "LLM left a TODO placeholder in the script block — "
        "check script_template post-processing in infer.py"
    )

def test_ext_args_present(tmp_path):
    """Generated script block uses task.ext.args."""
    source = ingest(Path("tests/fixtures/modules/chain_input"))
    functionalities = discover(source)
    spec = infer(source, functionalities[0])
    main_nf = render_module(spec)
    assert "task.ext.args" in main_nf, (
        "Generated module does not use task.ext.args — "
        "check ext_args enforcement in infer.py or generate.py template"
    )

def test_version_capture_present(tmp_path):
    """Generated script block writes versions.yml."""
    source = ingest(Path("tests/fixtures/modules/chain_input"))
    functionalities = discover(source)
    spec = infer(source, functionalities[0])
    main_nf = render_module(spec)
    assert "versions.yml" in main_nf, (
        "Generated module does not write versions.yml"
    )

These tests are intentionally narrow — they assert invariants, not exact output.
A test that asserts exact channel names or exact script content would be too
brittle to be useful. The invariants tested here are the ones enforced by
post-processing guards; if a guard is removed, these tests catch it.

Run with:
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... pytest -m llm -v

docs.title: Prompt 27 — Standards Drift Detection
docs.text:
Goal: A scheduled-friendly test that detects when the live nf-core module template diverges from nf_core_standards.json. Reports divergences as warnings (not hard failures) so CI doesn't break on upstream changes — but humans are notified before the drift silently degrades generation quality.

No new source files. This prompt adds tests/test_standards_drift.py only.

Implement tests/test_standards_drift.py.

All tests in this file are marked @pytest.mark.network and are skipped in
offline CI runs.

The reference for nf-core module structure is the official module template:
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nf-core/modules/master/modules/nf-core/MODULE_TEMPLATE/main.nf

And the environment.yml template:
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nf-core/modules/master/modules/nf-core/MODULE_TEMPLATE/environment.yml

--- HELPERS ---

def fetch_template(url: str) -> str:
    import urllib.request
    with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=10) as r:
        return r.read().decode()

--- TESTS ---

@pytest.mark.network
def test_versions_topic_channel_present_in_template():
    """nf-core template still uses topic: 'versions' on the versions emit."""
    template = fetch_template(MAIN_NF_TEMPLATE_URL)
    assert "topic: 'versions'" in template or 'topic: "versions"' in template, (
        "nf-core template no longer uses topic: versions — "
        "update nf_core_standards.json and Jinja2 template accordingly"
    )

@pytest.mark.network
def test_ext_args_pattern_present_in_template():
    """nf-core template still uses task.ext.args pattern."""
    template = fetch_template(MAIN_NF_TEMPLATE_URL)
    assert "task.ext.args" in template, (
        "nf-core template no longer uses task.ext.args — "
        "check whether the convention has changed upstream"
    )

@pytest.mark.network
def test_container_block_structure_unchanged():
    """nf-core template container block still uses singularity/docker ternary."""
    template = fetch_template(MAIN_NF_TEMPLATE_URL)
    assert "workflow.containerEngine == 'singularity'" in template, (
        "nf-core container block structure has changed — "
        "update the container Jinja2 template in templates/"
    )

@pytest.mark.network
def test_conda_channels_order_matches_standards():
    """Live environment.yml template conda channel order matches stored standards."""
    from code_to_module.standards import get_standards
    template = fetch_template(ENV_YML_TEMPLATE_URL)
    standards = get_standards()
    # Parse channel order from template
    import yaml
    parsed = yaml.safe_load(template)
    template_channels = parsed.get("channels", [])
    assert template_channels == standards.conda_channels, (
        f"Conda channel order has changed upstream.\n"
        f"Template: {template_channels}\n"
        f"Stored:   {standards.conda_channels}\n"
